texto: I sat taller, to suppress my impatience. It was infuriating, this waiting. I was thirty-seven years old. And like a child, an infant, really, I was at the mercy of others. Hour after hour of my life was spent waiting.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Rosie Sultan: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de sentido da existência e experiência vivida. O trecho “I sat taller, to suppress my impatience. It was infuriating, this waiting. I was thirty-seven yea…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que familia, helen-keller, waiting pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de waiting.
